title: 19th Annual Saxon Graham Lectureship with Dr. ...

So we're we have many things to be proud
of our Dr. Freud for.
>> Very distinct honor to introduce Dr.
Graham Culitz um who is the niece gain
professor of surgery chief of the
division of public health sciences and
associate director of prevention and
control at the siteman cancer center all
at Washington University School of
Medicine in St. Louis Missouri. Um,
Graham received his medical degree from
the University of Queensland in
Australia and M. PH and DRPH degrees
from the Harvard Chan School of Public
Health. He was on the faculty at Harvard
Medical School where he had a leadership
position in the nurse's health study
before going to Washington University.
Graham is a preeminent leader in cancer
prevention globally. He has more than
1,500 publications that have been cited
more than 400,000 times. It's like
mind-boggling to me. Making him one of
the medical researchers with the highest
impact in the world. He's made
significant contributions identifying
risk factors for cancer and other
chronic diseases and has led important
initiatives to translate the most
up-to-date scientific knowledge and make
it accessible for prevention for cli
clinicians, individuals, populations
um and particularly for underserved
people. He was instrumental in the
development of a website that provides
personalized, understandable advice
regarding chronic disease prevention
based on an individual's risk factors.
He led an initiative to involve the
broader St. Louis community in health
prevention with a notable initiative to
increase screening for breast cancer.
That program resulted in a measurable
decrease in um advanced stage breast
cancer among black women in St. Louis.
And most recently, his work has included
the integration of AI models using
mammograms to accurately assess breast
cancer risk in diverse populations,
allowing more personalized screening and
risk management.
Graham, and you notice there's a
throughine here of the name Graham, um
has a significant record of important
contributions to mentoring of students
and faculty in epidemiology and cancer
prevention. Notably, he served for 16
years as an external adviser for our UBN
NCI cancer funded Nci funded cancer
epidemiology training program. He's a
member of the National Academy of
Medicine, is a life fellow of the Royal
Australasian College of Physician and
serves on the NIH Council of Councils.
He's been awarded the most prestig
prestigious honors bestowed by the major
organizations in his field. The American
Cancer Society, the American Association
for the Advancement of Science, and the
American Association for Cancer
Research. With a long-standing interest
in the causes and prevention of chronic
disease and in his focus on man
mentoring, Dr. Culitz has shaped the
field of epidemiology in ways that
impact research and public health
practice worldwide. His focus on a
deeper understanding of cancer
prevention extends the kind of work that
Sax and Graham conducted decades
previously. So, please give a warm
welcome to our 26 Sax and Graham
lecturer, Dr. Graham Culitz.
Thank you.
Volume okay? Yeah. Good. Um, thank you
Joe Pauline and your great staff for
making this visit such a delight and
it's a real pleasure and honor to be
here uh to give this Saxon Graham

Um so institutional support can have a

After that first pregnancy, risk goes
